revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bo…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}} }} '''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]]（[[三宅島]]）にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすげのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、[[御笏神社]]に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、[[后神社 (三宅村)|后神社]]の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、[[東伊豆町]]片瀬の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『[[三宅記]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神 * [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager
{{DEFAULTSORT:かたすけしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{Otheruses|[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) の神社|[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}} {{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}} }} '''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすげのみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、[[御笏神社]]に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、[[后神社 (三宅村)|后神社]]の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、[[東伊豆町]]片瀬の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『[[三宅記]]』</ref>。

== 外部リンク == * [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神 * [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

* {{ill|Kokuhei Shosha (Engishiki)|WD=Q135206465}}

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|片菅神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Katasuge Shrine
| native_name = 片菅神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none
| map_type = Japan

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 3 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) の神社|[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}} {{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}} }} '''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、[[御笏神社]]に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、[[后神社 (三宅村)|后神社]]の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、[[東伊豆町]]片瀬の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『[[三宅記]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神 * [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager
{{DEFAULTSORT:かたすけしんしや}} [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[片菅神社]] to [[Katasuge Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{Otheruses|[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) の神社|[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}} {{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}} }} '''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、[[御笏神社]]に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、[[后神社 (三宅村)|后神社]]の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、[[東伊豆町]]片瀬の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『[[三宅記]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神 * [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

==End Japanese==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{Otheruses|[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) の神社|[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[東伊豆町]]片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}} {{神社 |名称 = 片菅神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[三宅村]][[神着村|神着]] |位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 片菅命 |社格 = [[式内社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan|label=片菅神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}} }} '''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[三宅村]] ([[三宅島]]) にある[[神社]]。
三宅島北東、周回道路である[[都道212号]]から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。
[[式内社]]である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、[[御笏神社]]に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、[[后神社 (三宅村)|后神社]]の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、[[東伊豆町]]片瀬の[[片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|片菅神社]]なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）
== 祭神 == * 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - [[事代主神]]（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（[[御笏神社]]の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『[[三宅記]]』</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神 * [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager
==End Japanese== [[Category:東京都島嶼部の神社]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{Otheruses|{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) の神社|{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}}

}}
'''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、{{jalink|后神社 (三宅村)|Q134930558|后神社}}の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）

== 祭神 ==
* 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

== 脚注 ==
{{Reflist}}

* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

'''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、{{jalink|后神社 (三宅村)|Q134930558|后神社}}の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===
* 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Otheruses|{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) の神社|{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の神社|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)}}

|名称   = 片菅神社
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=東京都島嶼部}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}}{{jalink|神着村|Q11590690|神着}}
|位置 = {{Coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 片菅命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 =

|神事   =

{{coord2|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}}
}}
'''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、{{jalink|后神社 (三宅村)|Q134930558|后神社}}の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===
* 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神
* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

{{DEFAULTSORT:かたすけしんしや}}

'''片菅神社'''（かたすげじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|三宅村|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|三宅島|Q1075076}}) にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

三宅島北東、周回道路である{{jalink|都道212号}}から外れて北に進んだ林の中に鎮座している。

{{jalink|式内社}}である'''片菅命神社'''（かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ）に比定されている<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - 八百万の神</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - 神社巡遊録</ref>。（ただし、他の論社として、{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}に合祀された勝祖(かつそ)神社（片菅命神社）や、{{jalink|后神社 (三宅村)|Q134930558|后神社}}の境内社/境外社である若宮神社、{{jalink|東伊豆町|Q304491}}片瀬の{{jalink|片菅神社 (東伊豆町)|Q134892414|片菅神社}}なども挙げられている<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。）

=== 祭神 ===
* 片菅命（かたすげのみこと） - {{jalink|事代主神}}（三島大明神）と后・佐伎多麻比咩命（{{jalink|御笏神社|Q110915859}}の祭神）の間の八王子の7番目の子「かたすげ」に比定<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>『{{jalink|三宅記|Q17215100}}』</ref>。

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===

* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Notes ===

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|7|8.1|N|139|32|46.3|E}}}}

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miyake Village|Q1154797}} ({{jalink|Miyake-jima|Q1075076}}), {{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{jalink|Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{jalink|Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{jalink|Higashiizu|Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{jalink|Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{jalink|Miyakeki|Q17215100}}''</ref>.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

* [https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|WD=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|WD=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|WD=Q304491}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|WD=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine|1=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

{{nihongo|'''Katasuge Shrine'''|片菅神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|WD=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine|1=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|WD=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine|1=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|WD=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|WD=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|WD=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.

'''Katasuge Shrine''' (かたすげじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|qid=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}} ({{ill|lt=Miyake-jima|Q1075076|qid=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}), {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}.

It is enshrined in woodland to the north, off {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo Metropolitan Route 212|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ring road in northeastern Miyake-jima.

It is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (かたすが[げ]のみことじんじゃ)<ref>[https://yaokami.jp/1131733/ 片菅神社] - Yaokami no Kami</ref><ref name="engi-chosa">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-01.html 片菅神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref><ref name="sh">[https://shrineheritager.com/katasuge-shrine/ 片菅神社] - Shrine-heritager</ref><ref name="jyr">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/shrine/engishiki/izu/ 伊豆國 九十二座] - Jinja Junyūroku</ref>. (However, other proposed sites include Katsuso Shrine (Katasuga-no-Mikoto Shrine), now merged into {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|qid=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}}; Wakamiya Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Kisaki Shrine (Miyake Village)|Q134930558|qid=Q134930558|lt=Kisaki Shrine|1=Kisaki Shrine}}; and {{ill|lt=Katasuga Shrine (Higashiizu)|Q134892414|qid=Q134892414|lt=Katasuga Shrine|1=Katasuge Shrine}} in Katase, {{ill|lt=Higashiizu|Q304491|qid=Q304491|1=Higashiizu}}<ref name="sh"/><ref name="jyr"/><ref name="engi-chosa2">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140116-02.html 片菅命神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.)

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Katasuge-no-Mikoto (かたすげのみこと) - identified as "Katasuge," the seventh child among the Eight Princes born between {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (Mishima Daimyōjin) and his consort Sakitamahime-no-Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishaku Shrine|Q110915859|qid=Q110915859|1=Oshaku Shrine}})<ref name="engi-chosa"/><ref name="sh"/><ref>''{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|qid=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}''</ref>.
